purpose: Determines appropriate historical reference points for this credit cycle by combining partial analogues rather than relying on any single-analogue framework.
components: 1989-91 S&L/CRE: closest institutional analogue for corporate-credit-led downturn (IG peaks ~200bp)
2005-07: transmission mechanism mismatch (household vs corporate leverage)
Japan 1990s: direction valid but 3x multiplier non-transferable
1998: partial analogue for external shock transmission
ARS vs SIV: competing CLO expiration analogues (gradual repricing vs panic collapse)
Historical deficit comparison table across 5 credit cycle peaks
current_assessment: 1989-91 is closest single analogue but no single analogue is adequate. Six features individually precedented but collectively unprecedented. Composite analysis required but methodology for weighting partial analogues is undeveloped.
limitations: No methodology exists for weighting partial analogues
Each analogue operated in fundamentally different institutional contexts
Unique combination may produce genuinely novel dynamics
Analogue analysis itself may break down for this cycle
